extraction.one_line_summary: FROOME Chris won the 40.2km time trial by 29 seconds over Kelderman, sitting 4th at first intermediate before accelerating mid-race to take command.
tactical_patterns: Froome deliberately paced conservatively through first split (23sec down, 4th) then unleashed full effort at mid-race checkpoint, revealing calculated energy distribution.
Contador rode exceptionally strong TT for final career individual effort, suggesting retirement motivation can temporarily override age-related decline in specialists.
Kelderman beat all GC rivals except Froome by significant margins, exposing him as underrated TT threat for stage race podiums.

rider_signals.FROOME Chris: positive: negative-split TT strategy (4th at first check, leader at second) shows superior pacing intelligence and indicates strong form before crucial mountain stage
rider_signals.KELDERMAN Wilco: positive: 2nd place 29sec down but significantly ahead of other GC riders suggests he's podium-caliber if mountain legs hold
rider_signals.CONTADOR Alberto: positive: 5th place in final career TT and jumped from 9th to 5th overall proves legacy motivation can produce outlier performances
rider_signals.NIBALI Vincenzo: negative: lost nearly 2 minutes to Froome and dropped from podium, confirming suspected weakness against clock
rider_signals.CHAVEZ Esteban: negative: lost over 4 minutes and collapsed in GC, revealing complete inability to sustain long TT efforts despite climbing prowess
